key_findings: ORS heartbeat self-monitor (C5.1) is the most critical missing control — ORS process failure is invisible without it, leaving all automated stability responses inert
Redis noeviction policy (C1.5) is required — LRU eviction silently destroys checkpoint data under memory pressure; controller must detect Redis errors and switch to SQLite WAL fallback
Neo4j unique constraint on Subdomain.name (C2.1) must be applied before first production write — MERGE without constraint allows silent duplicate nodes under concurrent ingest
ClickHouse FINAL keyword absent from reconciliation queries (C3.2) is a HIGH-probability gap — ReplacingMergeTree dedup is not visible without FINAL; mismatch rate appears artificially low
Eight named failure signatures (FS-01 through FS-08) defined — each has distinct observable metric pattern distinguishable from similar failure modes before catastrophic SLO breach
AWSEM concurrency caps enforced at dispatch level (C4.4) is non-negotiable — advisory caps alone allow Oracle CPU overload under burst conditions
Oracle tool timeouts (C7.3) are critical — nuclei without timeout on large-response targets causes OOM kill cascades in Enrich lane
Alert deduplication (10-minute window per signal) prevents Mattermost flood under sustained degradation; without dedup, operator alert fatigue causes missed CRITICAL signals
Hardening checklist has 47 items across 8 layers; 16 are CRITICAL and must complete before first production write
Failure signature library accelerates operator pattern recognition — named patterns (DNS Queue Starvation, DLQ Cascade, etc.) reduce mean time to identification from 30 min to < 5 min

hardening_layers.name: Checkpoint Store (Redis / SQLite WAL)
hardening_layers.highest_risk_failure: Redis running without AOF — all checkpoints lost on Redis restart — full corpus replay required with duplicate ingest risk

checks.check: Redis RDB snapshots disabled or secondary only
checks.action: AOF supersedes RDB for WAL use

checks.check: Redis bound to localhost or VPN interface only
Redis maxmemory set with noeviction policy
checks.action: Checkpoint data must NOT be evictable

checks.check: Stale PENDING checkpoint ORS monitor active
SQLite WAL fallback tested and validated
checks.action: Synthetic Redis kill test; verify WAL records checkpoint
AOF-only for single node; replication for HA

hardening_layers.name: Neo4j Graph Database
hardening_layers.highest_risk_failure: Missing unique constraint on Subdomain.name — MERGE creates duplicate nodes silently under concurrent DNS+HTTP writes on same subdomain

checks.action: CREATE CONSTRAINT subdomain_name_unique IF NOT EXISTS
Index: name, key_hash, ingested_batch_id, scanned, kril_rank_pct
pagecache.size=8g — prevents cold query latency spikes
neo4j/neo4j default changed before first write
All MERGE queries have explicit timeout at controller level
Daily Neo4j dump to cold storage

hardening_layers.name: ClickHouse Telemetry Store
hardening_layers.highest_risk_failure: ClickHouse background merge falls behind under high INSERT rate — parts accumulate — FINAL queries slow from seconds to minutes — validate lane backs up

checks.action: version = Unix timestamp; verify clock sync

checks.check: FINAL keyword enforced in all reconciliation queries
checks.action: Code audit: grep COUNT(*) FROM subdomains without FINAL

checks.check: Max insert block size tuned (1M rows)
Remote access restricted to controller/oracle IPs
checks.action: Bind ClickHouse to controller/oracle IPs only

hardening_layers.name: AWSEM Scheduler and Queue System
hardening_layers.highest_risk_failure: AWSEM concurrency cap as advisory only — dispatcher issues 400 tasks instead of 250 — Oracle CPU spikes — tool failures — DLQ cascade — all lanes degrade simultaneously

checks.check: Queue max depth enforced at enqueue (reject not drop)
checks.action: Rejection triggers backpressure to scan tool
Expired items logged and discarded; not silently retained

checks.check: Dead-letter visibility timeout configured (manual re-enqueue only)
checks.action: DLQ items must NOT auto-re-visible to primary queue

checks.check: Concurrency caps enforced at dispatch level (not advisory)
checks.action: AWSEM must not dispatch beyond concurrency_cap

checks.check: Per-lane queue depth monitored by ORS
Task deduplication at enqueue (within TTL window)
checks.action: Reject duplicate task IDs; prevents double-dispatch under retry
On restart, in-flight tasks reclaimed from checkpoint store
DNS DLQ cannot affect HTTP queue processing

hardening_layers.name: ORS Reflex Monitor
hardening_layers.highest_risk_failure: ORS process crashes silently without heartbeat monitor — escalation storms, CPU overloads, DLQ cascades proceed without automated response

checks.action: Test: fire reflex twice — verify same system state
Same alert not sent more than once per 10 min
After reflex fires, suppress triggering alert for 30 min

checks.check: All ORS thresholds in ors_config.json (not hardcoded)
ORS static fallback table in AWSEM
checks.action: If ORS fails, AWSEM applies safe defaults (suspend all lanes)
ORS monitors Neo4j, ClickHouse, Redis responsiveness

checks.check: Reflex audit log (signal, threshold, action, timestamp)
checks.action: Every reflex action logged with full context
Log reflexes but don't execute — for threshold calibration

hardening_layers.name: Controller Process
hardening_layers.highest_risk_failure: Controller has no memory limit — enrichment output buffering causes RAM spike — OOM — ingest halts — checkpoint in PENDING state indefinitely — no alerts

checks.check: Write order contract enforced as code assertion
Checkpoint write before datastore write enforced as code assertion

checks.action: Only controller process UID can write to artifacts/quarantine/

checks.check: Max concurrent connections to Neo4j enforced
checks.action: Connection pool max set in controller

checks.check: Controller memory limits set (systemd MemoryMax or Docker --memory)
Graceful shutdown handler (SIGTERM → flush → checkpoint → exit)
FQDN normalization at single entry point
checks.action: Single normalize_fqdn() function; no inline normalization

checks.check: Structured logging with correlation IDs (batch_id, lane, timestamp)
Source file hash verification enabled (SHA-256; HALT on mismatch)
checks.action: Reject chunk if hash mismatch; do not warn

checks.check: Retry limits enforced at controller level (no unbounded retry loops)

hardening_layers.name: Oracle Scan Node and Tooling
hardening_layers.highest_risk_failure: Nuclei without timeout on large-response target — Oracle OOM — nuclei OOM-killed — enrichment items silently lost — DLQ grows — no alert if Oracle output rate not monitored

checks.check: Scan tool versions pinned in deployment manifest
checks.action: dnsx, httpx, nuclei, katana: no 'latest' tag
dnsx: 30s; httpx: 30s; nuclei: 5 min per target

checks.check: Tool output written to temp dir (atomic move on success)
Oracle output JSON schema validation at controller
Tool process memory limits (ulimit or cgroup)
checks.action: Nuclei OOM-kill should not take down Oracle node

checks.check: Nuclei template updates managed (not auto-update during 72h)
Oracle work directory cleanup after batch
Rate limiter per tool per domain

hardening_layers.name: Network and Credential Security
hardening_layers.highest_risk_failure: Credentials in source code committed to git — all database access compromised; requires immediate rotation of all secrets

checks.check: Controller-Oracle on private network or VPN
checks.action: No traffic on public internet; private LAN or WireGuard
Native protocol TLS or HTTPS interface

checks.check: Redis TLS or UNIX socket (same-host preferred)
checks.action: UNIX socket preferred: lower latency, no network attack surface

checks.check: All credentials in secrets manager or env file (not source code)

checks.action: All DB passwords rotated; tested quarterly

checks.check: Mattermost webhook URL treated as secret
checks.action: Webhook URL in env file; not in logs or source
Password auth disabled; only controller public key authorized

checks.check: Audit log for credential access (alert on > 3 failed auth/min)

failure_signatures.name: DNS Queue Starvation
failure_signatures.description: Oracle cannot drain the DNS result queue fast enough

signature_metrics.pattern: LOW (Oracle idle — queue empty, not full)

failure_signatures.distinguish_from: DNS tool failure (CPU=0 AND queue full). Starvation shows empty queue + idle Oracle
failure_signatures.reflex: Check Oracle connectivity; verify dnsx process running; restart Oracle heartbeat monitor

failure_signatures.name: HTTP Rate-Limit Storm
failure_signatures.description: Target infrastructure rate-limiting httpx; Oracle backing off

signature_metrics.pattern: growing slowly (rate-limited items retrying and failing)

failure_signatures.distinguish_from: HTTP tool failure (DLQ grows fast and CPU is zero)
failure_signatures.reflex: Reduce HTTP concurrency by 30%; increase 429 cooldown to 120s; alert Mattermost

failure_signatures.name: Ingest Saturation
failure_signatures.description: Controller cannot write to Neo4j fast enough; ingest queue and checkpoint PENDING accumulating
failure_signatures.reflex: Reduce Neo4j concurrent transactions 15 → 10; reduce batch size 500 → 200 ops/tx; alert

failure_signatures.name: ClickHouse Dedup Lag Spike
failure_signatures.description: ClickHouse background merge not keeping pace with INSERT rate; FINAL queries slowing

signature_metrics.pattern: oscillating (spikes then resolves as merge catches up)

failure_signatures.distinguish_from: Real data mismatch (which does NOT resolve on its own)
failure_signatures.reflex: Force OPTIMIZE TABLE subdomains FINAL; reduce ClickHouse INSERT rate; alert

failure_signatures.name: DLQ Cascade
failure_signatures.description: One lane DLQ accumulates → downstream lane starves → multiple lanes degrade in sequence
failure_signatures.reflex: Investigate Lane X DLQ root cause first; do not treat downstream lanes in isolation

failure_signatures.name: Oracle Memory Pressure
failure_signatures.description: Nuclei or katana processing large-response target → buffering into Oracle RAM → approaching OOM
failure_signatures.distinguish_from: Oracle CPU overload (CPU high; memory stable)
failure_signatures.reflex: Reduce enrich concurrency 25 → 10; add nuclei output size limit; alert; investigate large-response targets in DLQ

failure_signatures.name: Checkpoint Store Failure
failure_signatures.description: Redis not persisting checkpoints; ingest continues without durable checkpoints — replay integrity violation

signature_metrics.pattern: rising for ALL batches (not just one)
still running (controller continues without checkpoint — DANGEROUS)

failure_signatures.distinguish_from: Single stale checkpoint (isolated to one batch_id)
failure_signatures.pre_catastrophe_window: IMMEDIATE — every ingest write without checkpoint is a replay integrity violation
failure_signatures.reflex: HALT ingest immediately; switch to SQLite WAL fallback; do not resume until checkpoint store confirmed healthy

failure_signatures.name: Neo4j Lock Contention
failure_signatures.description: Multiple concurrent MERGE transactions competing on same Subdomain node (DNS + HTTP simultaneous writes)

signature_metrics.pattern: variable (some batches fast, some very slow)

failure_signatures.distinguish_from: General Neo4j slowness (uniformly elevated latency, not variance)
failure_signatures.reflex: Reduce Neo4j concurrent transactions 15 → 5; add exponential backoff retry; investigate hotspot Subdomain nodes

validation_strategy.pass_condition: No CH write on Neo4j failure

risk_model.title: ORS process fails silently without heartbeat monitor
risk_model.mitigation: C5.1 (ORS heartbeat) is the primary mitigation; without it, ORS failure is invisible

risk_model.title: Redis evicts checkpoint data under memory pressure
risk_model.mitigation: C1.5 (noeviction policy); verified via Redis CONFIG GET maxmemory-policy

risk_model.title: Neo4j constraint missing allows silent duplicate nodes
risk_model.mitigation: C2.1 applied before first write; verified via SHOW CONSTRAINTS

risk_model.title: ClickHouse FINAL missing in reconciliation query
risk_model.mitigation: C3.2 code audit; add FINAL to all reconciliation queries

risk_model.title: Oracle tool timeout not set; runaway nuclei blocks Enrich lane
risk_model.mitigation: C7.3 enforced at Oracle invocation level

risk_model.title: Controller credential in source code committed to git
risk_model.mitigation: C8.5 verified before first commit; gitignore enforced

risk_model.title: Alert flood during sustained degradation
risk_model.mitigation: Alert dedup window (C5.3) prevents Mattermost flood

recommended_actions.action: Implement ORS heartbeat watchdog (C5.1) — most dangerous gap; ORS silence is invisible without it
Set Redis noeviction + verify AOF before first production write (C1.1, C1.5)
Apply Neo4j schema constraints (C2.1) and verify with SHOW CONSTRAINTS
Code audit: FINAL in all ClickHouse reconciliation queries (C3.2)
Set per-tool timeouts on Oracle (C7.3): dnsx 30s, httpx 30s, nuclei 5 min
Set Neo4j transaction timeout 60s (C2.5) and max connections 50 (C2.11)
Implement failure signature detection in ORS for FS-01 through FS-08
Add alert deduplication (10 min window) and reflex suppression (30 min) to all ORS alerts
Enforce firewall rules: only controller/oracle IPs accepted on ports 7687, 9000, 6379 (C8.9)
Run hardening checklist verification before each 72h operation window start

assumptions: A1: ORS has an API surface for heartbeat emission; external watchdog (cron + HTTP check) is acceptable
A2: Redis version supports AOF and noeviction maxmemory-policy (Redis 3.0+)
A3: Neo4j version supports schema constraints (Neo4j 4.0+)
A4: ClickHouse version supports FINAL keyword and ReplacingMergeTree (ClickHouse 21.6+)
A5: Mattermost webhook is available for ORS alert routing
A6: Oracle exposes system metrics (CPU, memory) accessible to Controller ORS via HTTP endpoint or SSH command
A7: Alert dedup implementation is at ORS level (not Mattermost); ORS tracks last-sent time per alert ID
